rows.Description:
SentiLink provides innovative identity and risk solutions, empowering institutions and individuals to transaction with confidence. We’re building the future of identity verification in the United States replacing a clunky, ineffective, and expensive status quo with solutions that are 10x faster, smarter, and more accurate.

We’ve seen tremendous traction and are growing extremely quickly. Our real-time APIs have helped verify hundreds of millions of identities, starting with financial services and rapidly expanding into new markets. SentiLink is backed by world-class investors including Craft Ventures, Andreessen Horowitz, NYCA, and Max Levchin.

We’ve earned recognition from TechCrunch, CNBC, Bloomberg, Forbes, Business Insider, PYMNTS, American Banker, LendIt, and have been named to the Forbes Fintech 50. We have also been named a 2026 FICO Industry Vanguard Decision Award Winner. Last but not least, we’ve even made history - we were the first company to go live with the eCBSV and testified before the United States House of Representatives on the future of identity.

SentiLink supports a variety of ways to work, ranging from fully remote to in-office. We operate as a digital-first company with strong collaboration across the U.S. and India. We maintain physical offices in Austin, San Francisco, New York City, Seattle, Los Angeles, and Chicago in the U.S., and in Gurugram (Delhi) and Bengaluru in India. If you’re located near one of these offices, we would love for you to spend time in the office regularly. Some roles are hybrid or in-office by design. For example, our engineering team in India works primarily from our Gurugram office.

Role:
SentiLink is looking for high-agency builders who don’t just want to join a role, they want to define one.

This is an open-ended, product-oriented hiring track for individuals who see an opportunity at SentiLink and want to pitch it. Rather than hiring into a predefined role, we are looking for people who can identify high-impact opportunities, articulate why they matter, and lead them from idea to execution.

As part of the process, you will be asked to propose a product, initiative, or new area of investment for SentiLink. We’re looking for candidates who combine strong product thinking, business acumen, and execution ability.

During the recruiting process, we will work with you to identify a product role where you can have the greatest impact based on your pitch, strengths and interests, as well as business needs.

Your pitch should answer:
What do you want to build at SentiLink?

Why do you think this would be compelling for SentiLink?

Why are you well-positioned to lead this?

What resources would you need to be successful?

What you’ll do:
If selected, you will take ownership of a high-impact product or business initiative and drive it from concept through execution. This may include:

Defining and validating new product opportunities

Translating ambiguous problems into clear product strategies

Leading cross-functional development across engineering, data, GTM, and operations

Owning product direction, execution, and outcomes

Partnering closely with leadership to prioritize and resource new initiatives

Building 0→1 products or scaling early-stage bets

This role is intentionally flexible, you may operate as a product leader, GM, or hybrid operator depending on the opportunity.

Examples of areas you might propose or lead:
New fraud or identity products

AI-driven product experiences or workflows

Expansion into new customer segments or verticals

Internal platform or tooling innovations

New GTM motions tied to product innovation

Who we’re looking for:
We’re looking for builders with deep experience in financial services, who are excited to move from identifying problems to building solutions.

You’re highly analytical, structured in your thinking, and able to break down ambiguous problems into clear opportunities. You’ve driven complex initiatives end-to-end, influenced stakeholders, and are comfortable using data to inform decisions.

You have strong product instincts: you can translate business problems into compelling product ideas and communicate them clearly across technical and non-technical audiences. You operate with high ownership, move quickly, and thrive in 0→1 environments.

Figma is growing our team of passionate creatives and builders on a mission to make design accessible to all. Figma’s platform helps teams bring ideas to life—whether you're brainstorming, creating a prototype, translating designs into code, or iterating with AI. From idea to product, Figma empowers teams to streamline workflows, move faster, and work together in real time from anywhere in the world. If you're excited to shape the future of design and collaboration, join us!

We’re looking for a strategic, entrepreneurial Product Manager to help shape product-led growth for our newest AI products. Figma has an ambitious, forward-looking roadmap that we’re rapidly executing on as product development and design rapidly change with AI. This role will help drive the company’s strategy for growth of these products and ensure users discover value in Figma as their workflows rapidly evolve. 

What you’ll do at Figma:
Partner closely with other product teams to develop and execute on a strategy for driving adoption of Figma’s newest and biggest bets.
Use data and insights to build a portfolio of experiments and new features that drive users to discover the transformative impact of Figma’s AI features and products
Shape Figma’s growth strategy as our business and product evolve.
Lead a fast-executing, impact-driven team. Collaborate deeply with Design, Engineering, and Product Leadership on both strategy and execution.
We’d love to hear from you if you have:
7+ years of product management experience
Familiarity with current AI-powered product development and a POV on how our industry will evolve
Experience leading major product or growth initiatives across user-facing products
Track record of delivering impact through data-driven experimentation and strategic bets
A knack for data and metrics, and strength in translating insights into strategy 
Are a strong cross-functional collaborator, both within the product development organization and with marketing / business functions
Have deep user empathy and are able to understand complex product spaces
At Figma, one of our values is Grow as you go. We believe in hiring smart, curious people who are excited to learn and develop their skills. If you’re excited about this role but your past experience doesn’t align perfectly with the points outlined in the job description, we encourage you to apply anyways. You may be just the right candidate for this or other roles.

About the job
Overview: The Product Manager will manage the Middle and Back Revenue Cycle portfolio across multiple IT organizations and align with strategic business goals across Middle and Back Revenue Cycle operational departments. Responsible and accountable for achieving outcomes from initiation to close-out in specified timeframes and delivering agreed upon product roadmap. Middle and Back revenue cycle includes Clinical Documentation, Charge Capture, Coding, Claims Creation and Submission, Denials Management, Payment Posting and Reconciliation, Patient Collections, Revenue Integrity.

Job Summary

This senior individual contributor role is primarily responsible for defining product strategy and vision, developing product roadmaps from ideation to launch, and making decisions on new products or enhancements to bring to market. This includes developing product enhancements based on validated learnings, serving as a facilitator for product teams and diverse stakeholder groups across the organization, and collaborating with operations teams and market stakeholders to support product adoption. This role is also responsible for performing internal and external discovery for new and existing products, validating solutions with end users, funneling learnings to product roadmaps, tracking outcomes and key performance indicators, and partnering with product team members to solve user problems.

Essential Responsibilities

Completes work assignments and supports business-specific projects by applying expertise in subject area; supporting the development of work plans to meet business priorities and deadlines; ensuring team follows all procedures and policies; coordinating and assigning resources to accomplish priorities and deadlines; collaborating cross-functionally to make effective business decisions; solving complex problems; escalating high priority issues or risks, as appropriate; recognizing and capitalizing on improvement opportunities. 
Practices self-development and promotes learning in others by proactively providing information, resources, advice, and expertise with coworkers and customers; building relationships with cross-functional stakeholders; influencing others through technical explanations and examples; adapting to competing demands and new responsibilities; listening and responding to, seeking, and addressing performance feedback; providing feedback to others and managers; creating and executing plans to capitalize on strengths and develop weaknesses; supporting team collaboration; adapting to and learning from change, difficulties, and feedback. 
Defines product strategy and vision, and develops product roadmaps from ideation to launch by making decisions on new products or enhancements to bring to market; evaluating potential business process changes, system impacts, and flow concepts to determine the viability of implementing desired enhancements; providing an end-to-end holistic view of how specific business processes and internal system configurations impact the user experience; developing business cases for new products, improvements to existing products, and/or business ventures; defining, managing, and avoiding technical debt as appropriate; developing product enhancements based on validated learnings; ensuring documentation clearly communicates customer/stakeholder needs to the product team; and working with team members to clarify features and requirements as necessary. 
Serves as a facilitator for the product team and diverse stakeholder groups across the organization throughout the product management life cycle by coordinating the resolution of inter-squad, cross-product and external dependencies, roadblocks and constraints; collaborating with operations teams and market stakeholders to support product adoption; coordinating training efforts to build readiness for production support and operational readiness teams; leading feature demos for stakeholders; providing product expertise, peer training, and consultation as appropriate; and adhering to enterprise compliance standards and managing technology risks appropriately. 
Performs internal and external discovery for new and existing products by collaborating with business partners to identify needs; identifying marketplace innovation; prototyping and validating with end users with support from UX design and research, engineering, and other stakeholders as appropriate; and funneling learnings to the product roadmap. 
Tracks outcomes and key performance indicators by defining and tracking KPIs and performance metrics (e.g., speed, quality, etc.); reviewing analytical dashboards and assessing KPI alignment; and developing and/or maintaining ad hoc reports, status updates, and presentation decks for key stakeholders. 
Partners with product team members to solve user problems by prioritizing the work backlog to ensure balance across new development, non-functional requirements, and technical work (e.g., maintenance, support, technical debt, etc.); and collaborating on competitive and user research/testing, experimentation, and/or design thinking initiatives. 

Knowledge, Skills and Abilities: (Core)

Ambiguity/Uncertainty Management
Attention to Detail
Business Knowledge
Communication
Critical Thinking
Cross-Group Collaboration
Decision Making
Dependability
Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion Support
Drives Results
Facilitation Skills
Health Care Industry
Influencing Others
Integrity
Learning Agility
Organizational Savvy
Problem Solving
Short- and Long-term Learning & Recall
Teamwork
Topic-Specific Communication

Knowledge, Skills and Abilities: (Functional)

Analytical Skills
Client Focus
Conflict Resolution
Cross Department Coordination
Debugging and Troubleshooting
Delegation
Demonstrating Personal Flexibility
Getting Work Done Through Others
IT Service Design and Introduction
IT Service Improvement
IT Standards, Procedures & Policies
ITIL (Information Technology Infrastructure Library) Consultation
Innovative Mindset
Managing Diverse Relationships
Mentoring and Coaching
Negotiation
Organizational Skills
Relationship Building
Risk Management
Technical Communication
Technical Documentation
Vendor Management

Minimum Qualifications

Bachelors degree in Computer Science, Business Administration, Information Systems Management, Data Analytics, Engineering, Social Science, or related field and minimum five (5) years experience in product management, project management, IT consulting, product development or support, business/system analysis, or a directly related field. Additional equivalent work experience in a directly related field may be substituted for the degree requirement. Advanced degrees may be substituted for a portion of the work experience requirements. 
Minimum two (2) years experience working with agile teams, including familiarity with scrum ceremonies and agile best practices. 
Minimum one (1) year experience in a leadership role with or without direct reports. 

Preferred Qualifications

Master's degree in Computer Science, Business Administration, Information Systems Management, Data Analytics, Engineering, Social Science, or related field. 
Three (3) years stakeholder management experience. 
Three (3) years experience working with product ideation and design thinking. 
Three (3) years experience implementing large or complex projects or programs, including designing and evaluating for scale based on the size of implementation. 
Three (3) years experience in business analysis. 
One (1) year healthcare experience. 
Two (2) years project management experience. 
Four (4) years experience conducting ROI analysis and quantifying value of business initiatives. 
Three (3) years experience in strategic planning and building product roadmaps. 
Two (2) years data analytics experience. 
Four (4) years experience developing and validating business cases, including identifying value of investment projects.

About the job
We are a global team of innovators and pioneers dedicated to shaping the future of observability. At New Relic, we build an intelligent platform that empowers companies to thrive in an AI-first world by giving them unparalleled insight into their complex systems. As we continue to expand our global footprint, we're looking for passionate people to join our mission. If you're ready to help the world's best companies optimize their digital applications, we invite you to explore a career with us!

Your opportunity

This role will be crucial to lead the Log Management product enterprise maturity that will directly contribute to the product adoption by the most prominent platform customers. The target enterprise capability set will position New Relic Log Management as a solution of choice for customers' strategic decisions around observability tools consolidation.

In this highly visible and strategic role, you will act as the full business owner for key areas of our Log Management product, taking proactive charge of strategy and execution. You'll be responsible for balancing the needs of a mature, business-critical product with the drive for bold innovation that solves the challenges of modern, multi-service architectures. If you are a high-agency leader who wants to make a massive impact in a fast-growing, dynamic company, this is an excellent fit for you!

What You'll Do

Lead the enterprise maturity strategy for the Log Management product, identifying gaps and opportunities to ensure sustained high ROI.
Construct a roadmap based on deep market research and customer interactions, with clear OKRs definitions.
Partner closely with cross organizational field, engineering, and design teams to collaborate on requirements discovery and product development. 
Effectively manage dependencies, priorities, and plans across multiple teams and functions across New Relic to ensure alignment to meet business goals
Develop a comprehensive understanding of the New Relic Log Management product, including its core strengths, functional gaps, and competitive market positioning.
Define a robust product strategy and release roadmap, ensuring the successful delivery of an initial suite of product capabilities.
Cultivate strategic partnerships with key customers to establish a feedback loop for requirements validation, continuous improvement, and long-term satisfaction.

This role requires

Bachelor's degree or equivalent practical experience.
A minimum of 2 years of experience in developing and successfully launching technical SaaS products.
Demonstrated technical proficiency in Log data, Logging solutions, and the common challenges within this domain.
Proven ability to lead and influence matrixed teams through expertise and impact rather than formal authority.
Possess exceptional communication skills, with a demonstrated ability to articulate complex technical designs succinctly or in-depth, tailored to specific audience needs and objectives.

Bonus points if you have

Possession of a background in Computer Science or a related technical field.
Demonstrated experience in the development or management of Log Management solutions.
Proficiency in leveraging AI tools to facilitate the generation of comprehensive product requirements.
Proven track record in developing AI-driven products tailored for technical user bases.

About the Role:

We are looking for a hands-on, execution-focused Senior Product Manager to join our Predictions team. In this role, you will be the dedicated PM for the CDNA exchange technology that powers our flagship "OG" product and various global brands. This is a critical role sitting at the heart of our B2C and B2B Predictions business.

You will report directly to the VP of Product and will be responsible for bridging the gap between high-level strategy and technical execution. We need a "do-er" who is comfortable owning the entire product lifecycle and thrives in a fast-paced, engineering-heavy environment.

Responsibilities:
Act as the primary Product Manager for the Predictions Exchange, ensuring the technology platform meets the needs of our global B2B and B2C markets.

Take full end-to-end ownership of product delivery, moving projects from conceptualization and requirement gathering to shipping and post-launch optimization.

Work closely with a large team of engineers to prioritize the backlog and ensure technical requirements align with business goals.

Collaborate with leadership to streamline the product development process.

Ensure the Predictions Exchange continues to scale as the backbone of our operations.

Requirements:
Proven Execution: You are a strong Individual Contributor with a track record of "getting things done" independently.

Domain Interest: A solid understanding of, or strong interest in, the sports industry is essential.

Product Lifecycle Mastery: Experience managing the full product lifecycle from concept to shipping.

Technical Familiarity: Ability to work effectively with large engineering teams and understand complex exchange/transactional technologies.

Communication: Strong ability to coordinate across different timezones (specifically Europe and US).

Adaptability: Comfortable working in a high-growth environment where changes happen quickly.

 
Bonus Points:
Experience with exchange technology or prediction markets.

Previous experience in the crypto or fintech space.

$160,000 - $230,000 a year

Job Description
This key role supports the execution of short-term and long-term transformation strategies, organizational governance, and strategic initiatives within the dynamic commercial automotive digital products sector, with a specific focus on Ford Pro Intelligence (FPI) and fleet software solutions. The EPM of Strategic Initiatives will be instrumental in tracking the success of FPI transformation initiatives, helping to bridge departmental gaps across Vehicle Cloud & Mobile Operations (VC&M), streamlining communication on objective progress, supporting organizational reporting, and maintaining robust processes to enhance overall efficiency and productivity. This position requires a blend of operational acumen, strong project management skills, and the ability to help translate leadership vision for commercial connected services into actionable plans. This role requires high emotional intelligence, the ability to work collaboratively cross-functionally, and the capacity to successfully navigate through the complex Ford Pro and broader Ford organization to support sustainable growth.

Responsibilities
Key Responsibilities:

Strategic Planning Support & Execution: Support the development and execution of strategies to achieve the company's goals within Ford Pro Intelligence, commercial connected vehicle solutions, and fleet data operations. Coordinate the implementation of strategic initiatives to optimize business processes and improve performance for commercial customers. Assist in data gathering and tracking for 2026 business planning activities specific to the FPI organization.
Organizational Governance & Operational Excellence: Support organizational governance, the rhythm of the business, and strategic planning to ensure consistency and operational efficiency across FPI and wider VC&M teams. Help identify gaps within departments, streamline communication, and maintain organizational reporting. Assist in tracking cost reduction, complexity reduction, and waste initiatives. Maintain dashboards and tracking tools for FPI strategic initiatives, Business Plan inputs, and funding strategies.
Program Management & Cross-functional Collaboration: Manage key workstreams and strategic projects from initiation to completion, providing essential project management and change management support. Collaborate with key business stakeholders to document, develop, and implement new processes and procedures. Participate in workshops and brainstorming sessions to align teams on strategic priorities. Support the tracking and coordination of the Ford Pro Intelligence Product Roadmap alignment.
Executive Support & Communication: Act as a communication liaison between internal teams (e.g., FPI Leadership, VC&M Functional Leaders, Ford Pro Business Office, Digital Product, Vehicle Programs, TPMs, EMs). Support corporate governance by assisting with meeting planning, agenda setting, note-taking, and tracking execution of action items. Distill discussions from team meetings into clear summaries and help prepare compelling presentations and reports for senior leadership. Assist in the preparation of materials for senior leadership and Board of Directors meetings. Manage daily/weekly project tasks, including ad-hoc assignments and confidential special requests.
Qualifications
Qualifications:

Bachelor’s degree, or an equivalent combination of work experience, relevant certifications, and training.
3-5+ years of progressive experience in program management, business operations, corporate strategy support, or a related role.
Solid understanding of the Ford ecosystem; experience in the commercial automotive industry, fleet management software, or Ford Pro is highly preferred.
Proven experience utilizing project management, change management, and process improvement methodologies.
Strong analytical and critical thinking skills, with the ability to assist in financial tracking and synthesize complex data into actionable insights.
Excellent communication, interpersonal, and presentation skills, with the ability to clearly convey complex ideas to various levels of the organization.
Ability to work independently, manage multiple project workstreams simultaneously in a fast-paced environment, and demonstrate meticulous attention to detail and high organization.
 


You may not check every box, or your experience may look a little different from what we've outlined, but if you think you can bring value to Ford Motor Company, we encourage you to apply!

At A-dec, we do more than create the highest quality products and services for the dental industry; we strive to deliver a superior employment experience for each of our team members. With an environment that encourages and assists each person in developing to their highest potential, a career at A-dec is incredibly rewarding.

Overview
The Product Manager II serves as a product portfolio and market expert, combining market/competitive analysis and customer feedback to establish and support a distinctive product roadmap for product enhancements and market releases. This role is responsible for strategic planning support, product development and launch, product lifecycle management, customer relationship development, and continuous improvement. May be responsible for highly complex products, platforms and/or strategic development partners.
Duties and Responsibilities:  

Work with product managers, designers, development teams and other stakeholders to form a deep understanding of customer problems and potential solutions.
Drive data driven decisions to improve product line financial performance. Balancing time spent internally for influence with externally for knowledge.
Make the right call on what needs to be addressed when and how value can be delivered as early as possible.
Difficult portfolio decisions that require long term vision and short term action.
Use research, analysis and decision-making skills to capture a wide range of enhancements and bug fixes and convert them into an achievable roadmap
Ensure development teams are clear about why features are prioritized and that they are motivated to build products for customers.
Work closely with Product Line Management Team to ensure alignment with the roadmap.
Engage regularly with other teams from the wider business, such as support, marketing, training and sales, who help bring your solutions to market.
Communicate with business stake holders and the development team to ensure our growth goals and objectives are aligned with strategic business objectives. 
Ensure transparency in our current workload, ensuring the business understands and has input into the project work backlog, soliciting feedback, and gathering information about potential future projects that can further increase the value we provide the business at large.
Own, organize, and update product feature backlog and establish priorities during planning and potentially daily stand ups.
Building relationships outside of product development; foster relationships with key business partners that benefit the ability to deal with issues and get better feedback.
Minimum Qualifications: 

Requires a bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Marketing, Business or other related discipline with approximately 10+ years of experience in product management, development, and/or analysis. 
Master’s in Business or equivalent
Requires excellent communication, presentation, and analytical skills. Demonstrates ability to communicate effectively both orally and in writing at all levels of the organization. 
High influence and respect throughout the organization.
Demonstrates good organizational skills and the ability to manage time and resources effectively. Humble, hungry, and smart.
Requires strong technical product knowledge, understanding of product strategy, and a high level of customer intimacy. Must also have strong knowledge of dental industry practices and trends. 
Demonstrates ability to work independently as well as in a team environment. Functional experience in engineering and product management (PO, PMM, PM). Expert PM with experience beyond single product.
Dental or medical device or consumable industry experience is highly desirable.
Ability to successfully work with cross-functional groups and multiple stakeholders along with experience taking a lead role on small projects or aspects of large scope projects.
Product Management training or certificate preferred.

We're transforming the grocery industry

At Instacart, we invite the world to share love through food because we believe everyone should have access to the food they love and more time to enjoy it together. Where others see a simple need for grocery delivery, we see exciting complexity and endless opportunity to serve the varied needs of our community. We work to deliver an essential service that customers rely on to get their groceries and household goods, while also offering safe and flexible earnings opportunities to Instacart Personal Shoppers.

Instacart has become a lifeline for millions of people, and we’re building the team to help push our shopping cart forward. If you’re ready to do the best work of your life, come join our table.

Instacart is a Flex First team

There’s no one-size fits all approach to how we do our best work. Our employees have the flexibility to choose where they do their best work—whether it’s from home, an office, or your favorite coffee shop—while staying connected and building community through regular in-person events. Learn more about our flexible approach to where we work.

Overview
Instacart's Platform team powers the foundational capabilities behind our marketplace and Storefront Pro, including order management, pricing and discounting, loyalty, payments, fraud, and customer experience. We're hiring a Senior Product Manager, Fraud & Trust to lead the strategy and execution of our fraud detection, identity verification, and appeasements platforms that protect Instacart's four-sided marketplace of customers, shoppers, retailers, and advertisers.

In this role, you will own the end-to-end product vision and roadmap for how Instacart prevents fraud, verifies identity, and resolves order disputes fairly. A core challenge is navigating the tension between cost efficiency and delivering a fast, fair customer experience - shaping fraud and appeasement policies with cross-functional partners that protect the business without degrading the customer experience that drives growth. You'll drive measurable impact on cost per order, fraud losses, and customer experience by evolving our real-time decision platform, advancing ML-driven fraud models, and shaping the next generation of AI-native fraud defense.

Our Fraud & Trust team directly drives cost savings, revenue growth, and enables new business launches across Instacart's marketplace and enterprise surfaces. If you thrive in a fast-paced, highly cross-functional environment and want to define the strategy for how one of the largest grocery platforms in the world fights fraud, this role offers the chance to deliver measurable impact.

What Makes This Team Unique
Instacart's Fraud & Trust team sits at the intersection of platform infrastructure and real-world commerce - protecting millions of transactions across a four-sided marketplace while serving as a growth enabler.

On the fraud and identity side, you'll own a real-time ML-powered decision platform and build the next generation of AI-native fraud defense. The industry is undergoing a generational shift from rules-based to AI-first fraud prevention, and you'll define Instacart's strategy for it.

On the appeasements side, you'll tackle one of the most interesting product challenges in commerce: how do you make customers whole quickly and fairly when something goes wrong, while maintaining thoughtful safeguards against abuse? Every customer-facing commitment we make builds the trust that drives loyalty and growth, and also requires careful product and policy design to manage cost responsibly. You'll design the systems and policies that solve this tension at scale, working across Order Quality, CX, Legal, and Finance.

About the Job
Own the fraud, identity, and appeasements product vision, strategy, and multi-year roadmap. Set a clear north star with quantified targets for fraud losses, cost per order, false positive rates, and customer experience - and regularly socialize that vision with leadership and cross-functional partners.
Drive the evolution of Instacart's real-time fraud detection and identity platform - including ML model strategy, decisioning, identity verification (selfie, passkeys, ATO prevention), and account integrity across both marketplace and Storefront Pro. Optimize the balance between fraud prevention and customer experience.
Champion an AI-native, AI-first approach to fraud and trust. Drive opportunities to apply AI/ML innovations across the full domain. Push beyond incremental rule tuning toward a fundamentally AI-first architecture.
Lead the appeasements and order-issue resolution strategy - defining policies, guardrails, and product capabilities that balance fair customer treatment with abuse prevention. Partner with Order Quality, CX, and Legal to shape policies where customer-facing commitments require thoughtful safeguards. Drive measurable improvements to efficiency.
Be the strategic leader, not just the executor. Proactively identify new opportunities, define the "where we go next" narrative, and produce clear product reviews that demonstrate depth, ambition, and clear trade-off analysis. Don't wait for partner teams to tell you what to build - own the direction.
Partner closely with Data Science, ML Engineering, and Fraud Operations to define PRDs, scope ML model improvements, and translate complex fraud signals into scalable product capabilities. Leverage operational insights while maintaining independence of judgment on strategic direction.
Collaborate across Platform, Payments, Order Management, Shopper, Legal, and CX teams to ensure fraud and trust capabilities are embedded across Instacart's full commerce stack - from checkout to post-order to international expansion. Stay ahead of the evolving fraud landscape, including organized fraud-as-a-service, AI-generated abuse, and emerging attack vectors.
About You
Minimum Qualifications
6+ years of product management experience, including 3+ years focused on fraud, trust & safety, risk, identity, or a related high-stakes platform domain.
Demonstrated ability to set product vision and strategy independently - not just execute on a defined roadmap, but define the north star, build conviction with leadership, and drive a multi-year narrative with measurable goals.
Proven track record driving measurable business outcomes in fraud prevention or risk management - such as reducing fraud losses, improving authorization/approval rates, lowering false positive rates, or optimizing cost per order.
Strong experience working with ML/data science teams on fraud detection models, risk scoring, or decisioning platforms. Comfort with data and experimentation (SQL, analytics tools, A/B testing) to size opportunities, validate hypotheses, and measure impact rigorously.
Demonstrated success working cross-functionally with engineering, operations, data science, finance, legal, and compliance - with strong written and verbal communication skills to produce compelling strategy documents and build confidence in direction.
Bachelor's degree in business, engineering, computer science, or a related field; equivalent practical experience considered.
Preferred Qualifications
Experience applying AI/ML innovations to fraud, identity, or trust & safety - such as shipping GenAI/LLM-powered products, building AI-native detection/decisioning systems, or leveraging graph neural networks and multimodal analysis. Strong preference for candidates who think AI-first, not rule-first.
Experience building for multi-sided marketplaces and/or enterprise/white-label platforms, with an understanding of how fraud controls need to adapt for different partner models and user types (customers, shoppers, merchants, advertisers).
Experience leading or influencing without direct authority in an operational environment (e.g., working with Fraud Ops, Trust & Safety Ops, or Risk Operations teams).

Quick Summary
Help create the future of claims at a fast-growing insurtech. 3+ years PM experience required. Agile, data-driven, and high-impact from day one.

Who We Are
Kin makes life simpler, more affordable, and better for homeowners — especially in the places where climate risks, rising costs, and outdated systems make it hardest. We start with smarter homeowners insurance and expand to everything homeowners need to thrive.

Using data, technology, and thoughtful human support, we're building products that are clear, fair, and help homeowners feel confident — so homeowners aren't left behind when they need help most.

Founded in 2016, Kin is a remote-first employer with Kinfolk across more than 35 states. We serve customers in 13 states (and counting). Our disciplined growth, strong customer satisfaction, and focus on long-term sustainability fosters outstanding growth, attracts marquee investors, and earns recognition and accolades, including:

Built In Chicago's Best Places to Work, Midsize Companies (2021-2026)

Forbes' America's Best Startup Employers (2026)

Inc. 5000 Fastest-Growing Private Companies

Forbes' Fintech 50 (2023-2026)

Great Places to Work Certified (2024-2026)

Most importantly, we're building Kin to be a place where people do meaningful work with real impact — for our customers, our communities, and each other. We're excited to tell you more about how you can contribute to our rapid growth, strong unit economics, profitability, and excellent customer ratings. To learn more about how we work and what we're building, visit kin.com and see how we work.

The Opportunity
We're looking for a Product Manager to reimagine the homeowners claims experience — one of the most consequential moments in a customer's relationship with Kin. When someone files a claim, they're often dealing with the worst day of their year. Our job is to make sure technology helps them get back to normal faster, and that the adjusters working on their behalf have the tools to do their best work.

This role sits at the intersection of customer empathy and technical depth. You'll own adjuster technology and customer-facing technology — building a data-driven roadmap, partnering with a dedicated engineering team, and influencing a cross-functional group that includes design, user research, and claims operations. If you're energized by complex domains with real human stakes and you want to drive innovation in a space that's long overdue for it, this is the role for you.

Overview: 
Responsible for developing and executing plans to defend the bank and our clients from fraud losses associated with the bank’s payment rails, products, and tools. Aligns closely with Product Managers, Operations, and IT partners to drive the investment in, and delivery of, highly effective fraud mitigation product and tools for our clients. The Senior Fraud Product Manager is accountable to drive the following key outcomes:

improve client experience 
reduce fraud losses
enhance and grow fraud product revenue 
ensure that our processes, policies, and capabilities are consistent with evolving industry norms and standards
Clarifying Responsibilities:

Establish an enterprise view of our fraud control investment portfolio and roadmap by coordinating with Product, IT (including Cyber Security, application owners, and agile teams), Operations (including Financial Crimes), and business leaders.  Effectively advocate for appropriate investments to address weaknesses and establish a best-in-class fraud defense program.

Work with these teams to improve internal processes associated with the execution and tracking of fraud events, including investigations, claims, and coordination of activities.

Demonstrated ability to communicate ideas and recommendations effectively in both business and technical language with clients, bank partners, and executive leadership
Experience incorporating client feedback into decision-making

Internal prevention and detection tools
Fraud control products which M&T offers to our clients (eg, Positive Pay)

Processes and playbooks to manage the bank’s response to fraud incidents from the point of discovery through resolution

rows.Role: Senior Product Manager, Trust & Safety, Integrity and Fraud
rows.Description:
About the Team
Our Trust & Safety, Integrity and Fraud Product team is dedicated to ensuring a safe and trusted experience for all customers across our platform, including Consumers, Merchants, and Dashers. We tackle complex challenges, from preventing fraud and ATO to ensuring authenticity, to building regulatory compliance capabilities, all while balancing risk mitigation and providing a seamless user experience. Our team partners closely with cross-functional groups, including Engineering, Data Science, Compliance, and Risk Operations, to drive strategic initiatives that protect our business while fostering growth.

About the Role
The DoorDash marketplace is expanding rapidly beyond just restaurants as we become “your door to more." As we add new products, geographies, ways for Dashers to earn, as well as use cases to help consumers save time, we’re expanding and enhancing our fraud capabilities to be best in the industry at keeping our platform safe and trustworthy. We strongly believe that trust is the most important attribute of an online marketplace, and the team ensures that we maintain this trust.

We’re looking for a Senior Product Manager that will lead dedicated cross-functional teams to solve complex challenges that impact Consumers, Dashers and Merchants and touch every corner of our business - from new user onboarding, to the in-app dashing experience, to the products and services being created that our users can’t find anywhere else. We are looking to hire for a few roles, so you’ll either lead a vertical fraud team protecting Consumers or lead a horizontal fraud platform team focused on building our Risk Engine, Data Signals Intelligence and Automation / Anomaly Detection capabilities. The Fraud team is in hyper growth in terms of its size and impact and offers a unique opportunity to set and execute on a vision that will shape the future of DoorDash.

You’re excited about this opportunity because you will…
Set the vision and long-term product strategy for one of our vertical fraud teams or our horizontal platform fraud team.
Develop and execute a customer-focused product roadmap through close collaboration with senior leadership, Operations, Data Science, Analytics, Design, and Engineering all while balancing business needs. 
Translate customer and internal-user needs into clear, well-defined functional and technical requirements backed by data analysis and deep understanding of our users
Guide and interface closely with data analysis and engineering teams to define scope, review and refine technical capabilities, prioritize projects for release, and define new opportunities.
Build long-term instrumentation, monitoring, and evaluation capabilities for product performance, tracking, and to create product insights. 
Establish business cases and projected return on investment to identify and prioritize opportunities
Partner with Finance and Business Leaders to manage the impact on the profitability of the overall business.
We’re excited about you because you have…
5+ years of product management experience in the tech industry
3 or more years of experience across Integrity/Trust & Safety/Fraud
Experience defining strategic vision and tactical execution in a given product area
Experience in iterative, agile software delivery, short release cycles
Focus and thoughtfulness on data and analytics to solve problems and experience achieving results through continuous improvement
Experience in effectively working with multiple teams for collaboration and company-wide project delivery
Tenacity to keep issues top of mind of relevant stakeholders and conviction to stand-by decisions
Time management skills and ability to prioritize independently
Bachelor or Master's degree in Computer Science or data-related domain
Must be comfortable regularly exercising discretion and independent judgment in performing job duties, including evaluating options, making informed decisions, and determining appropriate courses of action within the scope of assigned responsibilities.

About the job
Overview

 As our  Vice President, Risk (Fraud Strategy & Analytics)  , you’ll help drive Concora Credit’s Mission to enable customers to Do More with Credit – every single day. 

The Impact You’ll Have At Concora Credit

Located in Beaverton, Concora Credit has built a stellar reputation in the consumer finance space over the last 25 years. We focus on our core competencies: excellent customer service, analytics, technology, underwriting, and execution in delivering financing products designed to meet the needs of consumers with less-than-perfect credit histories. We offer our credit products at very competitive rates and simple terms and deliver exceptional customer service. We’re focused on growth, and we need your help to succeed!

We hire people, not positions. That's because, at Concora Credit, we put people first, including our customers, partners, and Team Members. Concora Credit is guided by a single purpose: to help non-prime customers do more with credit. Today, we have helped millions of customers access credit. Our industry leadership, resilience, and willingness to adapt ensure we can help our partners responsibly say yes to millions more. As a company grounded in entrepreneurship, we're looking to expand our team and are looking for people who foster innovation, strive to make an impact, and want to Do More! We’re an established company with over 20 years of experience, but now we’re taking things to the next level. We're seeking someone who wants to impact the business and play a pivotal role in leading the charge for change.

Responsibilities

 As our  Vice President, Risk (Fraud Strategy & Analytics),   you will: 

 Lead the fraud strategy analytics and modeling team to enhance Concora Credit’s capabilities in the fraud risk space. 
 Guide, coach, and motivate the team, consisting of different skill levels from Analysts to a Director. 
 Own fraud strategy for identity fraud and for existing account fraud (transaction fraud, payment fraud, and account takeover) in the credit card and private label business space. 
 Have a strong sense of ownership guided by the objective of minimizing fraud losses while driving continuous customer experience improvements. 
 Design and enhance the fraud strategy roadmap for the organization. 
 Partner with risk leadership, different business and operational organizations, and drive rapid change across the organization. 
 Effectively be on top of and communicate the state of fraud and updates/recommendations to key stakeholders. 
 Be well-versed in the latest data, modeling techniques, technologies, and solutions effective in fighting fraud. 
 Evaluate and implement new data/solutions to improve fraud defenses. 
 Have cutting-edge knowledge of all fraud-related items (past, present, and how the future is likely to evolve) in the consumer credit space. 
 Be familiar with statistical/ ML modeling techniques leveraged in fraud risk modeling and be able to guide the data scientists and analysts towards optimal outcomes. 

 These duties must be performed with or without reasonable accommodation. 

We know experience comes in many forms and that many skills are transferable. If your experience is close to what we're looking for, consider applying. Diversity has made us the entrepreneurial and innovative company that we are today.

Qualifications

 Requirements: 

 Bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience. Advanced degree in Statistics, Econometrics, Mathematics, Engineering, Financial Engineering, Operations Research, Physics, or Technology preferred. 
 10+ years’ experience in the consumer lending industry. 
 5+ years of hands-on experience with fraud strategy and analytics, preferably in the consumer credit space. 
 5+ years of experience/familiarity with statistical analysis tools and data software languages such as SAS, R, Python, SQL, and Excel. 
 5+ years of experience managing a team with analysts and data scientists. 
 Solid understanding and knowledge of retail or consumer lending businesses. 
 Very strong analytical, strategic and white space thinking skills. 
 Ability to communicate effectively and influence others across functions.

About Crunchbase
Crunchbase is a predictive solution that provides intelligence on private companies, powered by the unique combination of live private company data, AI, and market activity from over 80 million users. We predict private market movements that matter to help investors, dealmakers, and analysts make the right decisions.

We are committed to fostering a positive, diverse, and inclusive culture by hiring for potential and embracing individuals with diverse perspectives, backgrounds, experiences, and skill sets. We value transparency and openness, believing that an inclusive environment strengthens our teams and enhances our products.

Crunchbase has a remote-first approach, and is open to hiring in residents of these states: California, Colorado, Illinois, Florida, Georgia, Massachusetts, Missouri, Nevada, New Jersey, New York, North Carolina, Oregon, Pennsylvania, South Carolina, Texas, Virginia, Washington, and Maine.

Our inclusive remote-first culture, competitive pay, and employee wellness benefits set us apart!

About the Role:
As the Product Manager, Monetization & Growth, you will own key product experiences that drive customer acquisition, conversion, monetization, expansion, and retention across Crunchbase's product-led growth motion.

This role is responsible for the systems, experiences, and business levers that help customers discover value, choose plans, convert, expand usage, administer teams, and remain engaged over time. Core areas of ownership include pricing and packaging, self-serve growth experiences, enterprise administration capabilities, billing and subscription experiences, and adjacent monetization opportunities.

This role combines strong product judgment, analytical rigor, and experimentation. You will partner closely with Engineering, Design, Revenue, Finance, and GTM teams to identify growth opportunities, optimize conversion funnels, and build scalable monetization systems that improve both customer experience and business outcomes.

Pricing & Packaging remains a foundational component of the role, but the broader focus is ownership of the product experiences and systems that drive sustainable growth and revenue.

What You'll Do:
Monetization Strategy & Product Ownership

Own product experiences and systems that influence acquisition, conversion, monetization, expansion, and retention

Develop and evolve monetization strategies across pricing, packaging, subscriptions, billing, and account growth experiences

Identify opportunities to improve customer value realization while driving business growth

Prioritize initiatives based on customer impact, revenue opportunity, and strategic alignment

Pricing, Packaging & Subscription Management

Lead the design, testing, and optimization of pricing and packaging models across products and customer segments

Own product requirements and execution for pricing, packaging, billing, checkout, upgrade, downgrade, and cancellation experiences

Partner with Engineering and Finance to ensure accurate implementation of monetization systems and policies

Monitor pricing performance and recommend changes based on customer behavior, market dynamics, and business goals

Self-Serve Growth & Conversion Optimization

Own self-service growth opportunities across the customer journey, including acquisition, onboarding, activation, conversion, and expansion

Identify and optimize friction points within key conversion funnels

Partner with Marketing and Revenue teams to improve growth experiences across web, product, and lifecycle touchpoints

Evaluate emerging opportunities such as AI search, answer engine optimization (AEO), and new customer acquisition channels

Enterprise Administration & Expansion Experiences

Own enterprise administration capabilities that support team management, user provisioning, permissions, billing administration, and account governance

Partner with Revenue and Customer Success teams to identify opportunities that improve account expansion and team adoption

Improve administrative experiences that support both self-service and sales-assisted customer motions

Data, Experimentation & Insights

Leverage analytics, experimentation, and AI-powered tools to understand customer behavior, usage patterns, conversion drivers, and pricing sensitivity

Define success metrics and monitor business performance across monetization and growth initiatives

Use data and customer research to identify opportunities and validate product decisions

Utilize AI-enabled tools to accelerate research, analysis, experimentation, and product development workflows

Cross-Functional Collaboration

Partner closely with Engineering, Design, Revenue Operations, Marketing, Finance, Sales, and Customer Success to deliver growth and monetization initiatives

Align stakeholders around priorities, success metrics, and business outcomes

Communicate product strategy, tradeoffs, progress, and results to technical and non-technical audiences

Performs other related duties as assigned.

What Crunchbase Offers:
Bachelor's degree in Business, Economics, Data Analytics, Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field preferred

5+ years of Product Management experience with ownership of monetization, pricing, growth, billing, subscriptions, or related product areas

Ideally includes owning payment or billing systems at meaningful scale (e.g. $10M+ ARR)

Experience optimizing self-service customer journeys, including onboarding, conversion, checkout, upgrade, expansion, and retention experiences

Bonus points for candidates who have architected and launched new pricing tiers or usage based models

Experience with subscription, usage-based, or hybrid SaaS business models

Experience leveraging experimentation, analytics, and customer research to drive product decisions

Experience working cross-functionally with Revenue, Finance, Marketing, and Customer Success teams

Experience with enterprise administration, account management, team management, or governance capabilities preferred

Domain expertise in SaaS, data platforms, sales intelligence, fintech, venture, or marketplace businesses preferred

Strong understanding of product-led growth, monetization, pricing, packaging, and subscription business models

Strong analytical skills with the ability to translate data into actionable product insights

Experience designing and optimizing customer journeys, conversion funnels, and growth experiences

Understanding of experimentation frameworks and data-driven product decision making

Strong product management fundamentals, including discovery, prioritization, delivery, and measurement

Ability to balance customer experience, business outcomes, and technical constraints

Demonstrated curiosity and comfort using AI tools to improve productivity, research, and decision making

Strong communication and stakeholder management skills

Ability to operate effectively in ambiguous and evolving problem spaces

About the job
This position is listed on behalf of a partner company, who manages all applications and next steps. Our partner is looking for a Product Manager based in the United States.

This role offers the opportunity to own and shape key product areas within a fast-scaling, AI-enabled healthcare workforce platform. You will drive end-to-end product strategy across core modules that directly impact how health systems schedule, staff, and manage clinical labor. Acting as a key bridge between customers, engineering, and business teams, you will translate complex operational challenges into scalable digital solutions. The environment is highly collaborative, data-driven, and deeply mission-oriented, focused on improving healthcare delivery outcomes. You will combine product discovery, execution, and customer engagement to deliver meaningful improvements to clinicians and healthcare organizations. This position is ideal for a product leader who thrives in fast-paced, high-impact environments and enjoys working at the intersection of technology, AI, and healthcare operations.

Accountabilities

Own a significant product surface within core platform areas such as scheduling, on-demand staffing, or vendor management systems, driving end-to-end product definition, roadmap execution, and performance against key KPIs.
Act as the domain expert by deeply understanding users, customer workflows, competitive landscape, and market trends to inform product direction and strategic positioning.
Drive product discovery using qualitative and quantitative methods to identify opportunities, reduce risks, and define high-impact solutions aligned with business objectives.
Leverage AI tools to rapidly prototype features, analyze product behavior, and support decision-making through data-driven insights.
Collaborate closely with engineering, design, marketing, and customer-facing teams to ensure seamless delivery, adoption, and continuous improvement of products.
Develop and communicate clear product roadmaps, while influencing stakeholders and ensuring alignment across the organization.

Requirements

5+ years of experience as a Product Manager, with proven ownership of B2B software products and measurable impact on product performance and business outcomes.
Strong experience in product discovery, roadmap development, and delivering scalable solutions aligned with company strategy and KPIs.
Proficiency in data analysis, including querying data, interpreting metrics, and translating insights into actionable product decisions.
Hands-on experience using AI tools for prototyping, analysis, and accelerating product development workflows.
Excellent communication and storytelling skills, with the ability to influence stakeholders and clearly articulate product vision.
Experience working in complex, cross-functional environments; healthcare or workforce management experience is a strong plus.
Strong analytical mindset, ownership mentality, and ability to balance speed, quality, and prioritization effectively.

Abnormal Security is looking for an ambitious and growth-minded Senior Product Manager to own a new high-priority, high-stakes product initiative within our core Cloud Email Security group — the flagship product area driving Abnormal's growth and market leadership.

Abnormal built the highest-efficacy AI-native email detection engine in the market. We help customers — from small and medium-sized businesses to Fortune 100 enterprises — stay safe from complex, cutting-edge cyberattacks that cause financial loss and disrupt business operations. Our AI-native approach to email security, account takeover detection, and adjacent use cases has made us one of the fastest-growing cybersecurity companies in the world.

In this role, you will have direct interaction with some of the largest enterprise customers in the world, setting strategy, driving execution, and helping Abnormal continue to protect customers from evolving and sophisticated attacks. If you are a technical PM who gets energized by hard infrastructure problems and wants to see your work translate directly into competitive wins and customer outcomes, this is the role for you.

What You Will Do

Own a high-priority roadmap in our core product area. Define and evolve a 9–12 month roadmap for a strategically critical initiative within Cloud Email Security, prioritizing against customer value, competitive positioning, and revenue impact. 
Drive end-to-end product delivery. Lead cross-functional execution across Engineering, Design, Data, Sales, and Customer Success — from problem framing and PRDs through launch, instrumentation, and iteration. This role requires both strategic thinking and hands-on operational execution. 
Solve technically complex customer problems. Run discovery with Fortune 500 enterprises to understand their operational realities and translate insights into clear problem statements and measurable outcomes. Go deep with Engineering on architecture and infrastructure tradeoffs that underpin the initiative. 
Shape competitive positioning and GTM strategy. Help the GTM team articulate why Abnormal is the right choice for enterprises looking to move beyond legacy solutions — contributing to customer-facing messaging, analyst conversations, and sales enablement. 
Own metrics and represent the product. Define and track KPIs including adoption, competitive win rates, and revenue impact. Serve as the domain expert in executive reviews, customer calls, and escalations. 
Serve as the domain expert for your product areas in customer calls, escalations, executive reviews, and roadmap discussions—communicating clearly, managing tradeoffs, and building trust across the organization. 
Lead in an AI-native way of working Use AI tools (e.g., ChatGPT/Claude, v0, analytics platforms) and rapid prototyping to accelerate discovery, research, documentation, and UX exploration—raising the standard for how product work gets done at Abnormal. 

Must-Have Qualifications

6+ years of product management experience in software, with a strong track record of owning complex technical product areas end-to-end in B2B SaaS or similarly technical domains. 
Proven experience working on deeply technical products, collaborating closely with engineering on architecture, scalability, or AI/ML–driven features, and reasoning through technical tradeoffs and constraints. 
Demonstrated ability to own product strategy, definition, and roadmap for a product line or multiple complex surfaces—including problem framing, opportunity sizing, prioritization, and success metrics. 
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with comfort using data (dashboards, experiments, or SQL/analysis in partnership with Data) to inform decisions and refine product direction in ambiguous spaces. 
Excellent communication and cross-functional leadership, with the ability to drive alignment across Engineering, Design, GTM, and executives, and to represent the product in high-stakes or customer-facing scenarios. 
High ownership, operational excellence, and attention to detail across documentation, planning, stakeholder management, and launch execution. 
A growth mindset and willingness to learn quickly in a fast-paced, AI-native environment, including integrating new tools and workflows into your day-to-day product practice. 

Nice-to-Have Qualifications

Background in observability, infrastructure, platform, or detection products, especially roles that required learning the architecture underneath the product surface. 
Experience in enterprise cybersecurity, security operations, or email security 
Prior experience as a software engineer, data scientist, or in another technical role, or equivalent hands-on experience with distributed systems or AI-driven products.
Familiarity with AI-native workflows and rapid prototyping tools (e.g., Claude/ChatGPT, v0, Figma) to speed up discovery, ideation, and validation. 
Experience working closely with GTM teams on competitive positioning, customer-facing messaging, and sales enablement 

Why You’ll Thrive at Abnormal

You’ll thrive in this role if you:

Want to own a strategically important product initiative and see your work directly tied to competitive wins and revenue. 
Are energized by technically complex, high-ambiguity problems and want the autonomy to define the approach, not just execute a backlog 
Value high trust and high autonomy, and are excited to help shape both product strategy and the broader product management craft as Abnormal scales. 
Want to work at the intersection of infrastructure, AI, and security — helping the largest enterprises in the world stay protected.

rows.Description:

Ethos is a leading life insurance technology company on a mission to protect families by democratizing access to life insurance and empowering agents at scale. With its robust three-sided technology platform, Ethos is transforming the life insurance experience for consumers, agents, and carriers alike. Ethos offers instant, accessible products and a seamless online process that requires no medical exams and just a few health questions; it eliminates traditional barriers, making it easier than ever for everyone to protect their families. Ethos is redefining how life insurance is bought, sold, and underwritten.

About the Role

We’re looking for a seasoned product manager to join our Consumer Product team. This team is responsible for building a delightful digital consumer experience, from onboarding users to helping them identify their families’ needs to purchasing the right insurance products to protect their families.

The ideal candidate will combine first-principles thinking, understanding of consumer needs, and creativity to envision delightful customer experiences. They’ll drive experimentation and strong execution to deliver results. We’re looking for an entrepreneurial product leader who is passionate about advocating on behalf of users and driving business growth, demonstrates a high degree of ownership, and is looking to join a team with a clear mission and purpose.

Build experiences that touch millions of online insurance shoppers. This is a very customer-focused role with the opportunity to deliver measurable and visible impact

Ability to work cross functionally with other team members, and key Engineering and Design stakeholders to build delightful product experiences

About the job
We’re currently looking for a Product Manager who will take a key role on our United States location-neutral team.

This individual will work alongside four other team members, while also collaborating cross-functionally with teams across the organization. They will translate customer needs and business goals into clearly articulated requirements, driving the development of products that acquire and retain merchants.

In this role, we value someone who leads with a clear understanding of the problem before reaching for the solution, operates with a high degree of self-sufficiency in a lean team environment, and brings enough technical and business depth to connect customer needs, partner constraints, and engineering execution into a clear path forward.

What You’ll Do

Discovery & Prioritization

Conducting merchant interviews and participating in reseller calls to understand pain points firsthand, while also synthesizing support tickets, feature requests, Pendo data, and usage metrics to build a complete picture of customer and partner needs.
Synthesizing discovery inputs into clear problem statements that align the team around what is being solved and why before solutioning begins.
Partnering with the broader product team to prioritize what gets built and when, with decisions weighted heavily toward features that drive revenue growth.

Roadmap Stewardship & Launch Readiness

Maintaining and executing against a product roadmap that balances new features, platform improvements, and technical debt.
Clearly communicating the reasoning behind roadmap decisions to engineering, marketing, operations, and partner teams so everyone understands not just what is being built but why.
Owning launch readiness by coordinating across teams to ensure messaging, documentation, partner communications, and engineering work are all aligned and complete before a feature ships.

Data-Informed Decision Making & Requirements

Using product analytics tools to analyze feature adoption and user behavior, and translating that data into a clear narrative that builds the case for what should be built, changed, or deprioritized.
Translating customer and business needs into clear specs, user stories, or PRDs that engineering can build from.

Cross-Functional Collaboration

Embedded in the engineering team's daily rhythm, attending standups, refinements, and sprint planning, and reviewing completed work to ensure it meets the intended product outcome before release.
Partnering with marketing to review content, walk through feature use cases, and ensure messaging accurately reflects what the product is designed to do.
Working with operations to share product requirements, review documentation, and train the team on how new features work so they can effectively support merchants after launch.
Collaborating with the partnership team to gather and incorporate feedback from resellers and acquirers, and to coordinate release timing and communications so external partners are informed and prepared for every product change.

What You’ll Bring To The Table

2-8 years of product management experience shipping in-house software.
A demonstrated track record of shipping software products from concept to release.
Curiosity and determination to find answers even if they aren't easily available, and the ability to operate comfortably in ambiguity while driving toward clarity.
A natural collaborator who can bring together people and ideas across engineering, marketing, operations, and partner teams to move work forward.
Strong written and verbal communication skills with the organizational discipline to manage multiple workstreams, deadlines, and stakeholders simultaneously.
Payments or fintech domain experience, familiarity with acquiring, processing, card network rules, or merchant services.
Comfort working with product analytics tools and the ability to use data to support and defend product decisions.

Who We Are

We are a leading payments technology company that empowers small businesses across the United States to accept payments seamlessly through our flagship product, SwipeSimple. We distribute our solutions primarily through a robust network of hundreds of merchant acquirers and independent sales organizations (ISOs), who in turn onboard thousands of merchants onto the SwipeSimple platform each month.

We are backed by private equity, including WestView Capital Partners, and are at an exciting inflection point in our growth journey. We have a strong foundation of profitable recurring revenue today, and are focused on scaling over the next three to four years through optimizing our core channel, accelerating our direct-to-merchant offering, expanding value-added services, and selectively entering new markets.

At Zillow, we’re reimagining real estate to make it easier to unlock life’s next chapter. This team builds the lending experiences, internal tools, and operational systems that support a more connected mortgage journey inside the Zillow experience. The work spans highly complex, regulated workflows across verification, underwriting readiness, closing readiness, production visibility, and integrations with both Zillow systems and third-party mortgage platforms. By combining technology, automation, and deep partnership with Operations, Engineering, Design, Data Science, Compliance, and vendor teams, we’re improving the speed, quality, and predictability of the mortgage process for both borrowers and the teams who support them.
About the role
We’re looking for a Senior Product Manager to help transform mortgage fulfillment through AI-first product thinking, operational rigor, and systems design. This role sits at the intersection of product, operations, and technology, with the opportunity to rethink how work gets done across people, software, and automation in a highly regulated environment. You’ll help turn fragmented, manual workflows into scalable, intelligent, and reliable systems that improve file quality, reduce effort, streamline handoffs, and create more predictable closing outcomes.

You will operate across the fulfillment lifecycle to identify pain points, define product strategy, and deliver solutions that improve how work is structured, surfaced, and executed across teams and systems. The work requires a product leader who is equally comfortable navigating complex operational workflows, partnering deeply with business stakeholders and technical teams, and balancing speed, quality, compliance, and scalability. This is an opportunity to shape the future of mortgage operations by building smarter workflows, stronger internal tooling, and better-integrated systems that drive measurable business impact.

You Will Get To
Lead AI-first product strategy for mortgage fulfillment workflows, operational tooling, and decisioning systems across the loan lifecycle, identifying where automation, intelligence, and human judgment should work together to improve operator productivity and borrower outcomes.

Redesign complex, multi-step operational processes to improve quality, speed, cost efficiency, and the predictability of closing outcomes, with a focus on reducing manual effort, simplifying handoffs, and minimizing avoidable failure points.

Build and evolve internal tools that support file setup, verification, conditions management, underwriting readiness, closing readiness, and production visibility, helping teams work with greater clarity, consistency, and control.

Define how work moves across people, systems, and handoffs so the right actions happen at the right time, with clear ownership, stronger workflow orchestration, and better visibility into file progression across the fulfillment lifecycle.

Partner closely with Operations, Engineering, Design, Data Science, Compliance, and external vendors to translate business needs and operational pain points into scalable, high-quality solutions that work in real-world production environments.

Drive integrations across Zillow systems and third-party platforms, including LOS, POS, underwriting, verification, credit, and closing tools, ensuring the broader ecosystem supports a more connected and reliable operator experience.

Use data to identify bottlenecks, define success metrics, prioritize opportunities, and measure the business impact of product investments, using insights to continuously refine workflows and improve throughput, quality, and predictability.

Ensure solutions are operationally sound, compliant, auditable, and built to scale in a regulated environment, balancing speed of execution with risk management, reliability, and long-term maintainability.

We are seeking a n Director, AI Operations to serve as the driving force behind AI transformation across our webcasting platform and business. This role is fundamentally about execution and adoption - standing up an AI-enabled code factory, and embedding and scaling intelligent capabilities directly into our products and workflows. Salary:

140K - 160K

What your impact will be:

Architect and operationalize an AI-DLC powered software factory that fundamentally accelerates GlobalMeet's development lifecycle - from sprint planning and code generation to automated testing, QA, documentation, and release processes.


Standardize and scale AI-augmented development practices across R&D teams to drive measurable improvements in velocity, quality, and consistency across our mission-critical webcasting platform.


Initial focus will center on operationalizing Anthropic Claude Code and related agentic development tooling within enterprise-grade engineering workflows.


2. Player-Coaching & Capability Development

Mentor and grow the current team's AI fluency, technical depth, and operational maturity - elevating individual contributors and building a bench of AI-capable talent within the organization.


Lead by example, demonstrating techniques through the development of key AI features and critical architectural changes.

Identify and operationalize opportunities to integrate AI capabilities directly into GlobalMeet's core platform - think intelligent audience analytics, automated event production workflows, real-time stream optimization, and smart compliance tooling for investor relations.

Establish governance frameworks for responsible AI-assisted software development, including human-in-the-loop approval models, auditability, security controls, and operational safeguards.

Partner with Security, Infrastructure, and Compliance teams to ensure enterprise-grade AI adoption aligns with data governance and regulatory requirements.


Ensure AI-enabled workflows maintain enterprise standards for resiliency, traceability, and operational integrity across mission-critical systems.

Continuously monitor the rapidly evolving AI landscape - models, frameworks, tools, and industry best practices - to ensure GlobalMeet remains at the forefront of applied AI innovation.


Evaluate emerging technologies for practical applicability and bring forward actionable recommendations that create competitive advantage, reduce cost, or unlock new capabilities across the organization.


6. Enterprise Change Leadership

Serve as the organizational change leader for AI adoption, developing and executing training, communication, and enablement strategies that embed AI into daily workflows across all functional teams.


Foster a culture of continuous innovation where AI is not a side project but a core operating capability.

At PwC, our people in software and product innovation focus on developing cutting-edge software solutions and driving product innovation to meet the evolving needs of clients. In product management, you will be the general manager of commercial and internal products. You will sit at the intersection of the business, user experience, and the technologies that solve our customer and end-user problems; confirming they meet market demands and drive business growth. You will design, develop and manage activities for a specific product or group of products from product definition and planning through production, release, and end of life.
